code:
# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START
<System>
<Role>You are a sentiment classification model for Amazon Home & Kitchen product reviews.</Role>
<BaseGuidelines>
    - Star rating scale: 1 = very negative, 2 = negative, 3 = neutral/mixed, 4 = positive, 5 = very positive
    - Output format: MUST include <Reasoning> (brief analysis) and <Rating> (1-5) tags
    - Task: Classify the overall sentiment of the review based solely on the review text
    - Base the rating on overall customer satisfaction, not on isolated phrases
</BaseGuidelines>

<DynamicRules>
    STAR LEVEL CRITERIA:

    1 Star (Very Negative):
    - Immediate return or safety hazard mentioned
    - Product failure within 30 days with negative resolution
    - Explicit warnings: "dangerous," "don't buy," "avoid"
    - Pattern: "Broke within [timeframe]" + negative conclusion

    2 Stars (Negative):
    - Core functionality failure despite initial promise
    - Multiple defects requiring workarounds
    - Regret language: "wish I hadn't bought," "false advertising"
    - Example: "Works BUT [critical flaw makes it unusable]"

    3 Stars (Neutral / Mixed):
    - Equal balance of praised features and functional limitations
    - Temporary/conditional utility: "good for guests," "better than nothing"
    - Requires substantial user effort for basic functionality
    - Non-essential flaws with intact core functionality

    4 Stars (Positive):
    - Explicit star deduction for specific non-core issues
    - Praised longevity/durability with minor usability complaints
    - Pattern: "Deducting one star for X but still works well"
    - Functional excellence overshadowing single convenience flaw

    5 Stars (Very Positive):
    - Transformative language: "exceeded expectations," "life-changing"
    - 3+ praised features outweighing any single drawback
    - Explicit "still 5 stars" declarations override specific critiques
    - Minor issues framed as afterthoughts to enthusiastic endorsement
    - Price praise + "worth every penny" without functional complaints

    EDGE CASE HANDLING:
    - Final assessment rule: Last substantive statement dictates rating
    - Deducted stars + recommendation →5 ("Deduct 1 star but still love")
    - Multi-update reviews: Prioritize most recent experience
    - Safety/return mentions →1 regardless of other positives
    - Core vs convenience: Functional defects →2-3, convenience →4
    - "Worth the effort" praise →5 if transformative, →4 if conditional
</DynamicRules>
</System>

<FewShotExamples>

Example 1 (New 5→4 Error Fix):
Review: "SOLVES my scraping needs perfectly! Deducting one star because not dishwasher safe."
<Reasoning>Explicit deduction for specific usability flaw without overriding endorsement.</Reasoning>
<Rating>4</Rating>

Example 2 (Key 4→5 Case):
Review: "Gets 4 stars for comfort but 5 overall - 3 amazing features make it exceptional!"
<Reasoning>Multi-feature praise overrides single critique via explicit rating.</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 3 (Durability 4-Star):
Review: "Love these towels! Deducting one star - lint issues but durability impresses."
<Reasoning>Specific flaw balanced by praised longevity. Maintains recommendation.</Reasoning>
<Rating>4</Rating>

Example 4 (Transformative 5-Star):
Review: "Revolutionized my kitchen! Minor size quibble irrelevant to performance."
<Reasoning>Transformative praise makes trivial drawback negligible.</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 5 (New 1→3 Fix):
Review: "Stopped working after 3 days but works when shaken. Not worth returning."
<Reasoning>Early failure but functional workaround. Mixed experience.</Reasoning>
<Rating>3</Rating>

Example 6 (Core Failure 2-Star):
Review: "Blade rusted after 2 uses. Makes great smoothies when it works."
<Reasoning>Core functionality compromised by material defect.</Reasoning>
<Rating>2</Rating>

Example 7 (Explicit 5-Star):
Review: "Not dishwasher safe but STILL 5 stars - best scraper ever!"
<Reasoning>Explicit 5-star declaration overrides convenience flaw.</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 8 (Temp Use 3-Star):
Review: "Great for guest baths! Lacks daily durability but cute design."
<Reasoning>Conditional utility with aesthetic appeal balances to neutral.</Reasoning>
<Rating>3</Rating>

Example 9 (Safety 1-Star):
Review: "Smoked upon arrival - immediate fire hazard! Return initiated."
<Reasoning>Safety hazard overrides all other considerations.</Reasoning>
<Rating>1</Rating>

</FewShotExamples>

<Task>
Review: {review}
# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
artifacts_json:
{"error_examples": "RULE COVERAGE:
  1 star: OK
  2 star: OK
  3 star: OK
  4 star: OK
  5 star: OK
  All categories covered.

ERROR DISTRIBUTION (31 total misclassified):
  gold 4 -> 5: 11
  gold 5 -> 4: 11
  gold 1 -> 2: 4
  gold 4 -> 3: 3
  gold 1 -> 4: 1

MISCLASSIFIED EXAMPLES (your prompt got these wrong):
  1. Review: "I like this towel. It is 100% Turkish cotton and measures 27 inches by 55 inches. The dobby weave is somewhat intricate so you are going to get some good absorbency. This towel also comes in a bath sheet, which is competively priced with the local st..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 4, 5] | Disagreement: 0.17
  2. Review: "EDIT: A year of use later it's still humming along and doing its job just fine. Sometimes it seems like it doesn't pick up quickly that it should be upping its speed on its own in the auto setting, but maybe it's just taking longer for the air to rea..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [4, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  3. Review: "UPDATE
==================================
We purchased this Procter-Silex toaster in 2013 and it lasted 3 years.  We were okay with that.

We purchased another one in January of this year, 2016, and already one side won't work.  Less than 2 months is..."
     Gold: 1 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [4, 4, 3] | Disagreement: 0.17
  4. Review: "I really like these potholders. They look great and do a good job of keeping hot pots and pans and other dishes out of the oven off the table and counters. They had a bit of a plastic smell when they arrived but after I washed them (they held up fine..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  5. Review: "I bought these mainly for sending leftovers. It is easier than sending my rubbermaid containers because I am not out much money if they don't come back."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [4, 4, 3] | Disagreement: 0.17
  6. Review: "I like the Stasher Snack bag. It is the perfect size for snacks on the go. I also like that you can store, cook, put in the microwave or even in the dishwasher! This is great for small treats. Don't try to overstuff. You don't want the seal to get fo..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  7. Review: "This really is a beautiful mug. It's so beautiful I am thinking of just using it as decor rather than actually using it. It's not really big enough to use for my morning cup of coffee (I need a BIG mug for that), although it's the perfect size for my..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17

BORDERLINE EXAMPLES (correct but workers disagreed):
  1. Review: "The towels are definitely cute and well made. Very attractive. The only reason Im not giving them five stars is because they aren't very absorbent. Out of the package they aren't really absorbent at all. Throw them in the wash with some fabric soften..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [3, 4, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  2. Review: "Over the past two months or so I've gotten numerous sets of kitchen towels and the process was always the same. Try them. See the weren't absorbent. Wash them numerous times with fabric softener to make them somewhat absorbent and keep at least four ..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  3. Review: "If there's anything you ever thought you wanted or needed in a trash can, this one has it. First, it's a semi-round, so it seems to have a smaller footprint than traditional round cans. Second, it has a silent opening and soft closing, which is great..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17

Summary: 31 errors, 12 borderline out of 80 examples."}
